title: Ionopsidium abulense

This family is part of the order Icacinales and the clade asterids, which also includes other flowering plants like lavender and sage. The family Icacinaceae consists of about 50 species, mostly found in tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and Africa. The exact classification of Ionopsidium abulense may vary slightly depending on the source, but its placement within the family Icacinaceae is widely accepted.

intro: Ionopsidium abulense, also known as Abul's Ionopsidium, is a rare and endangered succulent plant species native to the mountains of northern Pakistan and eastern Afghanistan. Traditionally, Ionopsidium abulense has been used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues.
